title: viola tricolor subsp. sabulosa
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. sabulosa, also known as wild pansy or heart's ease, is a flowering plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). Traditionally, Viola tricolor subsp. sabulosa has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and supporting respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Viola tricolor subsp. sabulosa typically involve infusions, tinctures, or topical applications, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
